findings.message: 4-week timeline for first-ever UE5+Cesium+dual-platform+screenshot-diff MVP is risk-on; SPEC must encode an explicit if-slip rule (D17/OQ-04).
Parallel v1 (Three.js) and v2 (UE5) evolution forces CI matrix work; existing Playwright suite must stay green throughout (D9, AC-V2-11).
USD 20/month hard cap on Google Tile API spend is the single largest external constraint; offline tile cache is mandatory for dev workflow (D3, OQ-05).
Dual primary platforms (Windows + Linux native, no Proton) doubles CI and screenshot-diff baseline work (D5, AC-V2-08/09).
Engine ladder Degrau 3 slot for aero-fighters-v2 is pre-authorized by foundation/SPEC.md; no constitution amendment required.
MVP scope reconciled as 'minimum-viable-combat slice of arcade-combat long-term identity' (Q1 + Q2 + Q9), not 'flight-only'.

decisions_required: OQ-01: Screenshot-diff tolerance metric and threshold (game-tester + game-designer to propose).
OQ-02: BP-to-C++ migration trigger value in concrete profiler units (software-architect to propose; game-developer to validate).
OQ-03: 1Password item ID for the GCP Tile API key (operator to provide).
OQ-04 / D17: Exact wording of the if-slip rule for week 3 (product-engineer synthesis draft; operator approves).
OQ-05: Offline tile cache strategy (devops-engineer + software-architect).
OQ-06: License status of Google Earth reference imagery used as screenshot-diff baselines (game-tester + product-engineer).
OQ-07: Time-of-day / sun position lock for screenshot determinism (game-developer + game-tester).
OQ-08: CI runner specification with GPU for both Windows and Linux lanes (devops-engineer).
OQ-09: Asset policy for UE5 (procedural vs FBX vs Quixel) — foundation is silent (game-designer).
OQ-10: Cesium for Unreal plugin version pin (software-architect + devops-engineer).
OQ-11: Aircraft model source — v1 procedural geometry is not directly portable (game-designer + game-developer).
OQ-12: Map coordinate system for AA gun placement (game-designer + game-developer).
OQ-13: Tag/branch naming convention distinguishing v1 and v2 builds (devops-engineer).
